proved: Reservation validates a 256-bit authorization capability plus bounded run, call, model, and unique frozen source IDs before touching authorization state.
One Rust authorization-registry mutex covers expiry, exact run/source-set/route checks, call-ID replay detection, and both budget decrements.
Eight parallel attempts against a four-call route produce exactly four successful reservations and four budget-exhausted results.
A successful call ID cannot be reused on another provider route, and failed scope attempts consume no budget.
Per-route and total remaining-call counters reach zero together for the approved two-route fixture.
An expired capability returns an explicit expired result and is removed from process memory without consuming a call.
Status serialization exposes remaining budgets but contains no question, label, excerpt, prompt, output, or credential.
No Tauri or MCP reservation command, provider executor, vault read, or network operation was added.
The compiled MCP reports the authorizer and internal reservation as separate truthful implementation states.
notProved: Reservation binds actual question, excerpt, task instruction, or cross-phase artifact bytes to the approved scope; it currently rechecks source identity only.
A reservation is consumed immediately before a vault read or network request; no authorized consumer exists.
Reserved capacity is refunded or finalized after transport failure; the executor lifecycle is not implemented.
The packaged native authorization dialog has been clicked or visually inspected.
A product workflow creates, monitors, persists, resumes, or revokes an adversarial run.
Live provider behavior, quality, cost, latency, retention, or panel superiority.
